summary.intent: Implement task #212: the framework-agnostic FocusScope core — a stateful utility that constrains keyboard Tab focus within a container, enabling focus trapping for Dialog, Drawer, Popover, and other overlay components.
summary.outcome: Full TDD implementation landed: new module at crates/ars-components/src/utility/focus_scope/mod.rs with 50 passing tests, 4 snapshot fixtures, 3 proptest invariants, 6 spec-conformance tests, 0 missed mutants. Spec synced to resolve named-effect drift. focus_next/focus_previous stubs removed per user feedback. Commit ready to push as PR after rebase onto main.

repo: The named-effect pattern (PendingEffect::named(Effect::Foo) markers + adapter dispatch) is the established agnostic-core convention for effects — calling use_platform_effects() synchronously from within agnostic-core transitions is architecturally impossible; Dialog and Popover already established the correct pattern.

Utility components follow a single-file module structure (no child files): live_region/mod.rs and dismissable/mod.rs are the canonical templates. Inline tests under #[cfg(test)] mod tests, snapshot fixtures in snapshots/ subdirectory.

spec/foundation/02-component-catalog.md:254 incorrectly lists FocusScope/FocusRing/LiveRegion/VisuallyHidden under ars-a11y; actual placement is ars-components::utility::*. This is a pre-existing workspace cataloging issue affecting four components.

GitHub issue #212 test 9 uses the attribute name data-ars-focus-trapped, but spec §2 specifies data-ars-trapped. The spec is authoritative; a dedicated regression test was added to pin the correct name.
ComponentPart derive uses the first variant as ROOT regardless of its name.

Synchronous DOM queries (e.g., get_tabbable_elements) cannot live in the agnostic core — the issue's 'Element/ref handling note' is explicit. Adapters call PlatformEffects::tabbable_element_ids directly.
cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener) triggers the cleanup captured at install time — no separate FocusTrapRelease effect variant is needed.
FocusNavigationOptions and focus_next/focus_previous must live entirely in the adapter layer: synchronous DOM lookups and live element handles make them impossible to implement in the agnostic core.

code.finding: Canonical example of named-effect pattern: Effect::RestoreFocus emitted via PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus), adapter dispatches.
Canonical example of named-effect pattern for focus effects in overlay components.
snapshot-count gate: cargo xtask ci snapshot-count validates that per-component snapshot budgets are met.
New module ~1300 LOC. Contains State/Event/Context/Props/Messages/Effect/Part/Machine/Api. Tests inline. Rc import dropped when FocusNavigationOptions/AcceptFn were removed per user feedback.

workflow: TDD discipline: write failing tests first (compile with placeholder impls), verify red, then fill in implementation until all pass — never write impl before the test baseline.

Post-implementation-audit skill is mandatory per CLAUDE.md before presenting for user review: Phase 1 = spec/impl drift, Phase 2 = external surfaces/adapter specs/catalog, Phase 3 = coverage + mutation triage.
Spec sync resolving known drift must land in the same PR as the implementation (CLAUDE.md policy).
User review checkpoint is required before running cargo xci --profile fast and committing/pushing.
cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <path> for targeted mutation testing; 0 MISSED is the acceptance gate.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject used to confirm no orphaned snapshot fixtures after any test removal.

When a proptest invariant fails, the fix is to make the invariant match the actual correct semantic (e.g., saved_focus is retained on Deactivate{restore_focus:true} by design) rather than weakening the test.
Coverage report parsing required multiple approaches; the most reliable was regenerating with cargo llvm-cov and grepping the annotated text output for the specific module.

friction: Spec §1.6 had significant architectural drift: transition closures calling use_platform_effects() synchronously from the agnostic core, which is impossible. Required understanding the existing Dialog/Popover patterns before the correct approach was clear.

SendResult does not expose a then_send accessor; understanding that queued events are processed synchronously and their effects merged into the same SendResult required checking the API shape before writing tests.
Coverage report parsing was difficult: required multiple grep/awk attempts to isolate uncovered lines in focus_scope from the full workspace report.
Issue attribute typo (data-ars-focus-trapped vs data-ars-trapped) required cross-referencing issue text against the spec and adding a dedicated regression test to prevent future confusion.
The worktree fell one commit behind main (Accordion #662 landed) during implementation, requiring a rebase before push.

First proptest invariant (activate-deactivate-inactive) failed because saved_focus is intentionally retained when restore_focus:true — required restructuring the invariant to assert the actual correct semantic.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter FocusScope components — tracked under epic #10's adapter tasks; DOM-level focus query, sentinel rendering, keydown handler, and FocusNavigationOptions live here.
FocusRestorationStack for nested scope tracking (spec §8) — adapter-scoped client-only state, deferred.
audit_focus_on_orientation_change (spec §1.6.1) — adapter-scoped, removed from agnostic-core spec; if normative, belongs in spec/foundation/11-dom-utilities.md §3.2.

spec/foundation/02-component-catalog.md:254 incorrectly lists FocusScope/FocusRing/LiveRegion/VisuallyHidden under ars-a11y instead of ars-components::utility — workspace-wide cataloging cleanup task affecting four components.
ars-leptos::hydration::setup_focus_scope_hydration_safe — pre-existing Leptos-specific hydration helper that operates independently of the new agnostic-core surface; tracked under task #196.
focus_next/focus_previous and FocusNavigationOptions are intentionally adapter-layer only; adapter tasks under epic #10 will implement them with real DOM access.
